briefs.title: BC Casinos Must Follow New Anti-Money Laundering Rules Starting April 2026
briefs.summary: A new regulation under BC's Gaming Control Act takes effect April 13, 2026, setting out formal definitions and rules for preventing illegal financial activity at provincial gaming facilities. It covers how operators must handle high-value casino chips (worth $5,000 or more), patron gaming accounts (accounts where players deposit money before playing), online gaming accounts, and dealings with money services businesses (companies that transfer funds, deal in cryptocurrency, or issue money orders).
briefs.action_required: Review your gaming facility's compliance program against the new Prevention and Detection of Unlawful Activities Regulation before April 13, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Prevention and Detection of Unlawful Activities Regulation takes effect
